title: Next.js Architecture

questions.question_text: What is the main difference between Next.js and React?
options: React is a library, Next.js is a framework that provides routing, SSR, and SSG out of the box
Next.js is a database, React is for UI
questions.explanation: Next.js builds on top of React to provide a complete set of tools for building production-ready applications.
SSR generates HTML for each request on the server, which is great for SEO and dynamic data.
SSG generates HTML at build time, resulting in extremely fast page loads.

options: Updating static pages in the background without needing a full rebuild of the site
questions.explanation: ISR allows you to have the benefits of static sites with the ability to update data on a schedule.

options: Components that only run on the client
Components that execute ONLY on the server, reducing the amount of JS sent to the browser
questions.explanation: Server Components help keep your bundle size small and improve performance.

questions.question_text: Which folder is used for the modern routing system in Next.js (version 13+)?
questions.explanation: The 'app' router uses a file-system based routing with folders and `page.tsx`/`layout.tsx` files.

options: To define the CSS of the page
To create a UI that is shared across multiple pages (e.g., Navbar, Footer)
questions.explanation: Layouts preserve state and don't re-render when navigating between sibling pages.

questions.question_text: How do you fetch data in a Next.js Server Component?
options: Using standard `fetch()` with `async/await` directly in the component function
You can't fetch data in components
questions.explanation: Next.js extends the native fetch API to include caching and revalidation.

options: The process where React attaches event listeners to the static HTML sent by the server to make it interactive
questions.explanation: Hydration turns static HTML into a live, interactive React application.

questions.question_text: What does the 'use client' directive do?
options: Marks a component as a Client Component, allowing it to use hooks like useState and useEffect
questions.explanation: Next.js components are Server Components by default; you must opt-in to interactivity.

questions.question_text: What is an 'API Route' in Next.js?
options: A backend endpoint written inside the Next.js app (usually in the /api folder)
questions.explanation: API routes allow you to build a full backend within your Next.js project.

options: Code that runs before a request is completed, useful for authentication or redirects
questions.explanation: Middleware runs on the 'Edge' and is extremely fast.

questions.question_text: Which component is used for optimized image loading in Next.js?
questions.explanation: The Image component automatically resizes and optimizes images for different devices.

options: Automatically loading the data for a linked page in the background as soon as it appears in the viewport
questions.explanation: This makes page transitions feel nearly instant.

questions.question_text: True or False: Next.js requires a separate Express server to run in production.
questions.explanation: Next.js has its own built-in server and can also be deployed as a static site or to serverless functions.
